ownership.boundary: The triage repository owns extraction and validation. AgentSwitchboard may vendor a pinned snapshot and render prompts but must not silently rewrite prompt IDs or execution-surface doctrine.

executionSurfaces.description: Instructions pasted into an interactive AI chat or coding-agent conversation.
Executable shell or PowerShell launch content that invokes GNHF with explicit bounds and an embedded or referenced objective.

variables.name: xyz_actionable_insights
variables.meaning: actionable insights or reusable findings to route
variables.example: prompt-kit registry extraction and selector logic
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_agent_command
variables.meaning: base executable GNHF or ACP will spawn

variables.name: xyz_app_context
variables.meaning: current application context, repository state, and operator constraints
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_app_name
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_artifact_root
variables.meaning: approved ignored output root for generated evidence

variables.name: xyz_audiences
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_available_evidence
variables.meaning: available docs, screenshots, repositories, logs, or runtime proof
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_bounded_queue
variables.meaning: finite ordered list of no more than five GNHF mutation items
variables.example: 1. schema parity; 2. stale-artifact guard; 3. technician launcher

variables.name: xyz_branch_pr_sprint
variables.meaning: known branch, pull request, or sprint identifier
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_candidate_repositories_or_org
variables.meaning: candidate repositories or organization to prioritize
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_constraints
variables.meaning: material safety, time, tooling, platform, or scope constraints
variables.example: no secrets; Windows-first; no workbook binaries in Git
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_context_path
variables.meaning: path to a context, plan, or handoff artifact
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_current_circumstances
variables.meaning: current blockers, urgency, available tools, and operator circumstances
variables.example: limited provider quota; clean main; overnight run desired
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_device_os_access
variables.meaning: devices, operating systems, and access available to the operator
variables.example: Windows 11 workstation with PowerShell 7 and WSL Ubuntu
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_evidence
variables.meaning: known commit, PR, report, or blocker

variables.name: xyz_experience_level
variables.meaning: operator experience level for the tutorial
variables.example: intermediate IT project manager and repository operator
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_failure_command
variables.meaning: exact failing test, validator, CI check, or workflow

variables.name: xyz_forbidden_scope
variables.meaning: what this chat must not touch

variables.name: xyz_gnhf_agent_spec
variables.meaning: exact GNHF agent specification proven by P37

variables.name: xyz_gnhf_run_id
variables.meaning: specific .gnhf run directory or ID when resuming

variables.name: xyz_interruption_reason
variables.meaning: known or suspected reason a GNHF run stopped
variables.example: Windows update restart / provider timeout / commit failure

variables.name: xyz_issue_sources
variables.meaning: read-only issue sources used to compile the queue
variables.example: gh issue list; failing tests; TODO.md

variables.name: xyz_local_path
variables.meaning: verified local application or repository path
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_repo_or_path
variables.meaning: repo full name or local path

variables.name: xyz_required_checks
variables.meaning: required checks that may not be downgraded to SKIP
variables.example: PowerShell parser; JSON schema; fixture E2E

variables.name: xyz_run_evidence
variables.meaning: screenshot, transcript, log, or artifact path

variables.name: xyz_runtime_sprint
variables.meaning: runtime-proof sprint name and exact behavior under test
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_source_material
variables.meaning: source prompts, notes, handoffs, or requirements to consolidate
variables.example: P01, P07, and current sprint handoff
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_source_prompt_or_path
variables.meaning: detailed sprint prompt, plan, or file path

variables.name: xyz_spawn_evidence_path
variables.meaning: external or ignored spawn-readiness evidence path

variables.name: xyz_target_artifact
variables.meaning: actual artifact or deliverable that must be produced
variables.example: versioned prompt-registry JSON and selector CLI
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_user_goal
variables.meaning: concrete outcome the user wants from the application
variables.example: select and render the correct prompt without manually browsing the workbook
variables.origin: registry override inferred from V38 prompt usage

variables.name: xyz_validation_order
variables.meaning: explicit test order or deferred checks
variables.example: targeted checks first; Pester and survey last
